reasoning: The informal statement says "H is generated by the set H-{1}", which means the subgroup closure of H\{1} equals H. The CORRECTED version captures this as `Subgroup.closure ((H : Set G) \ {1}) = H`. The ORIGINAL instead writes `closure ((H : Set G) \ {1}) = ⊤`, claiming this closure equals the whole group G (the top subgroup). This is mathematically false for any proper subgroup H (e.g., H = {0,2} in Z/4: the closure of H\{identity} equals H, not the whole group). The two statements are incomparable in general — they only coincide when H = ⊤. The error is a misreading of the informal statement: the author replaced "equals H" with "equals the whole group", apparently confusing the role of H as subject with H as the subgroup meant to be generated.
